perturbation.original_text: Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be the property of Ingram, and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement or Ingram's request, be immediately returned to Ingram.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be the property of Ingram, and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement, Vendor shall have the option to either return or destroy Proprietary Information within thirty (30) days. Ingram's request for return of Proprietary Information must be made within 10 days after expiration; otherwise, Vendor is only obliged to ensure destruction.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction by giving Vendor the option to return or destroy the information, while the original clause mandates immediate return to Ingram upon expiration or Ingram's request. This creates ambiguity about Vendor's obligations and Ingram's rights regarding the Proprietary Information upon termination. The original agreement states it should be immediately returned, this change says 'the option to either return or destroy', creating contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be the property of Ingram, and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement, Vendor shall have the option to either return or destroy Proprietary Information within thirty (30) days. Ingram's request for return of Proprietary Information must be made within 10 days after expiration; otherwise, Vendor is only obliged to ensure destruction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives Vendor the 'option to either return or destroy Proprietary Information,' whereas the original agreement and the contradicted text stated that it should be 'immediately returned to Ingram'. The change creates a contradiction as the vendor is no longer obligated to return it, creating an inconsistency as per the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, as different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: For purposes of enforcing this provision, Vendor's return obligation shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of enforcing this provision concerning the Proprietary Information, Vendor's return obligation shall not survive the termination of this Agreement if Vendor adheres to a strict protocol of secure deletion of all information within 60 days as verified by a third-party auditor at Vendor's expense. This audit protocol will supersede any other stipulations regarding the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause states that Vendor's return obligation survives termination. The modified text states it *does not* survive if a certain secure deletion and audit protocol is followed, creating a contradiction. This creates uncertainty about the actual obligation of Vendor after termination. The original text specifically mentions the return obligation will survive, this change states it will not, thereby contradicting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination of this Agreement by Ingram, Vendor shall return all Proprietary Information to Ingram within thirty (30) days, irrespective of format. However, if terminated by Vendor, Vendor is permitted to retain a copy of the Proprietary Information for archival purposes, provided it is not used for commercial gain. For purposes of enforcing this provision concerning the Proprietary Information, Vendor's return obligation shall not survive the termination of this Agreement if Vendor adheres to a strict protocol of secure deletion of all information within 60 days as verified by a third-party auditor at Vendor's expense. This audit protocol will supersede any other stipulations regarding the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states Vendor's return obligation *shall* survive termination, while the changed text states it *shall not* survive under specific conditions. This creates a direct contradiction, as the changed text introduces an exception that negates the original unqualified statement regarding the survival of the return obligation. This contradiction is present within the Contradicted Text, as the changed text is included in the section that was previously only affirmative and non-conditional, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: The rights, promises, duties, and obligations set out herein, and the validity, interpretation, performance, and legal effect of the whole Agreement shall be governed and determined by the laws of the State of California.
perturbation.changed_text: The rights, promises, duties, and obligations set out herein, and the validity, interpretation, performance, and legal effect of Sections 1 through 3 of this Agreement shall be governed and determined by the laws of the State of California, whereas, Sections 4 through 6 shall be governed and determined by the laws of the State of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by specifying that different sections of the agreement are governed by the laws of different states. The original text states that the entire agreement is governed by California law. The modification splits the governance, stating that sections 1-3 are under California law while sections 4-6 are under Delaware law. This is a direct contradiction and makes it uncertain which state's laws apply to the agreement as a whole and how to resolve conflicts. This change creates in-text contradiction, as the original text specified the entire agreement being governed by the laws of California.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The rights, promises, duties, and obligations set out herein, and the validity, interpretation, performance, and legal effect of Sections 1 through 3 of this Agreement shall be governed and determined by the laws of the State of California, whereas, Sections 4 through 6 shall be governed and determined by the laws of the State of Delaware. In the event that any provision is found invalid or unenforceable pursuant to Domestic Distribution Agreement March 15, 1996 Form 403 Ingram Micro (3/15/96) 108 <PAGE> 13 statutory or Judicial decree, such provision shall be construed only to the maximum extent permitted by law, and the remainder of the Agreement shall be valid and enforceable in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text specifies that the entire agreement is governed by the laws of California. The changed text states that sections 1-3 are governed by California law, while sections 4-6 are governed by Delaware law. This creates a clear conflict regarding which state's laws apply to the agreement as a whole, satisfying the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning that the contradiction is introduced in the same place as it is being checked, further establishing that the change itself is creating the contradiction.
